title: T-066 — Docs: TEC-031 Regulatory Compliance Dashboard prototype gap brief
prompt:
# T-066 — Docs: TEC-031 Regulatory Compliance Dashboard prototype gap brief

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §9.3, §9.5 TEC-031, §4A TEC-031
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Brief unblocking TEC-031 prototype work.

Document each regulation tile (data source + threshold), the per-FG flag table contract, and the ATP/cleaning evidence cross-link to 09-QUALITY.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- design/specs/TEC-031-regulatory-dashboard.md [create]

## Out of scope
- Prototype JSX
- Implementation

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the brief, when reviewed, then it lists 7 regulation tiles, their data sources, and threshold rules.
2. Given the brief, when read by a UX designer, then it is sufficient to draft the prototype.

## Test strategy
- Manual review by Quality Lead.
- No automated tests for docs.

## Risk red lines
- Do not bind hard data sources that are not yet shipped.
- Do not claim the screen is blocked by missing prototype; implementation is covered by the paired spec-driven UI task.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
